behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (boundary): this failure appears at module handoff (collectors -> report assembler -> summary input), not inside one pure function. Rationale 2 (mock risk): unit-testing a merge helper can pass while orchestration forgets one source; wiring tests catch that. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about "content passed" (pre-generation payload), so no real model judgment is required (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow), and no requirement for real network/OS behavior is stated (disqualifies System).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/assembly modules with stub adapters for the 3 sources, run one report build, and assert the payload sent to summary-generation contains fields from metrics endpoint, issue tracker, and deployment log.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (boundary): resilience behavior is in interaction between source adapter error handling and report composition. Rationale 2 (mock risk): unit tests of formatter logic miss whether upstream exceptions are converted into section-level fallback notes. Rejected higher levels: the behavior does not require real network outage mechanics, only handling an unavailable source condition (disqualifies System); no dependence on model output quality (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with one source adapter configured to raise an unavailable error while the other two return data; assert run completes, report contains `data unavailable` note only for that section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (boundary): correctness depends on generation quality (faithful representation of key values), which is a model decision. Rationale 2 (mock risk): deterministic stubs always return expected text and cannot reveal hallucination/omission drift from the real model. Rejected higher level: behavior text targets one summary step's quality, not multiple agentic stages in sequence (disqualifies Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed input datasets with known key values, call the real summary model, and score outputs for factual inclusion/consistency against required values over repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (boundary): this is end-to-end delivery across real external interfaces (source APIs/email transport) and inbox receipt, which is beyond in-process wiring. Rationale 2 (mock risk): mocked email clients can pass while SMTP/API auth, routing, or provider-side delivery failures break real inbox delivery. Rejected higher levels: behavior text checks successful delivery path ("reaches ... inbox correctly"), not model output quality criteria or multi-agent decision chains (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow as minimum).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in a staging-like environment against real endpoints (or production-like test endpoints) and a real mailbox, then verify recipient inbox receives the report with expected structure/content envelope.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Rationale 1 (boundary): failure is state handoff across modules (delivery failure -> fallback persistence -> next-run retry), which is broader than one function. Rationale 2 (mock risk): pure unit tests can miss persistence/reload bugs across invocations; integration with real local filesystem catches this. Rejected higher levels: behavior can be triggered by injected transport-unreachable errors without real network semantics (disqualifies System as minimum), and it does not depend on model judgment or multiple agentic decisions (disqualifies Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run invocation 1 with delivery adapter returning unreachable error; assert report file is written to local filesystem and marked pending. Run invocation 2 (simulated next schedule) with delivery restored; assert pending report is retried and sent, and local pending state is cleared.
